goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Hey Ameena,

You said you feel pretty low this morning (45), and looking at your data, I see why. Last night you only got 4.6 hours of sleep—that's almost an hour less than your usual average, and your bedtime was unusually early at 1:00 AM. Your body's recovery signals (HRV 127.9, resting heart rate 61) are actually strong, which creates a weird mismatch: your physiology is recovering well, but your mind and sleep are struggling.

Yesterday, stress was elevated at 55 and climbing, and your mood stability was critically low at 24, even though your coping capacity was high. You also had a major shift in routine: you finished all your eating by 9:00 AM, which is hours earlier than normal. That early eating window paired with the early bedtime suggests your whole daily rhythm got pushed forward, which can really throw your system off.

On the positive side, your focus yesterday was maxed out at 100 and still rising—you're clearly engaged and capable even when tired. Your social score was also perfect. That high engagement amid rising stress and workload is a classic pattern; you're meeting demands but it's taking a toll.

Given all this, today is about **rebuilding your rhythm**. The goal is to gently pull your eating and sleep timing back toward your normal patterns while managing the stress that's accumulating.

You've got the capacity to focus intensely; today's about using that focus to protect your basics.

assumptions: User can take brief pauses during work
User has access to protein-rich foods
User can adjust evening schedule
User has phone/alarm device
soft_constraints: caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits
maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep
consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend

generated_actions.title: Set a 9:30 PM 'lights out' reminder on your phone
generated_actions.rationale: Last night's sleep was severely truncated at 4.6 hours, a -1.52 fast z-score, despite an unusually early bedtime (1:00 AM). Sleep duration is declining (-0.38 slope). Given your low self-report (45) and 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority, an external cue to start your wind-down earlier can help you protect more sleep opportunity tonight, directly addressing the 'sleep_debt' risk flag and your sleep goal domain.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did your 9:30 PM reminder help you get to bed earlier to aim for more sleep?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the reminder to initiate their bedtime routine earlier

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
generated_actions.description: Set a recurring alarm for 9:30 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your cue to get into bed and start winding down, aiming for lights out by 10:00 PM.

must_avoid: starting a new task after the alarm
using bright screens after the alarm

generated_actions.title: When you feel overwhelmed during work, pause for 2 minutes of 4-7-8 breathing
generated_actions.rationale: Your stability status is poor: stress was elevated yesterday (55) and rising sharply (+6.11 slope), workload is high (65) and rising, and you reported mixed emotions (Annoyed, Frustrated, Tired, Excited) with critically low mood stability (24). With hard constraints 'tight schedule' and 'high workload', this micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel overwhelmed') to disrupt stress accumulation and address the 'volatility' risk flag.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 4-7-8 breathing technique when you felt overwhelmed today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports using the breathing technique at least once when stress or overwhelm arose

assumptions: You can take brief pauses during work
generated_actions.description: If you notice stress, frustration, or feeling overwhelmed building during your high-workload day, immediately stop and do 3 rounds of 4-7-8 breathing: inhale 4 seconds, hold 7, exhale 8. Focus only on your breath.

must_avoid: continuing the stressful activity while breathing

generated_actions.title: After your morning coffee, plan a protein-rich lunch to eat by 1:00 PM
generated_actions.rationale: Nutrition confidence is low (0.57) and while protein was adequate yesterday (100.7g), your eating pattern was highly unusual with all meals finished by 9:00 AM. Habit-stacking lunch planning to your morning coffee routine addresses the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag, the soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits', and aims to stabilize energy by preventing a long afternoon fast.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did planning your protein-rich lunch after coffee help you eat better and earlier today?
evaluation.success_definition: Daily protein intake reaches at least 90g (addressing moderate confidence) AND user reports the planning was helpful

assumptions: You have access to protein-rich foods
You can plan your lunch
generated_actions.description: Right after you finish your morning coffee, decide on a lunch that includes a substantial protein source (like grilled chicken, fish, tofu, or lentils) and aim to eat it before 1:00 PM local time.

generated_actions.title: After 60 minutes of seated work, take a 5-minute walk or gentle stretch break
generated_actions.rationale: Activity profile shows concerning declines: MVPA was very low yesterday (34 min) and sharply declining (-8.45 slope), steps are below baseline and falling (-584 slope), and sedentary ratio is high (0.61). This tiny break directly addresses the soft constraint 'consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend' within your 'tight schedule' and 'high workload' hard constraints, leveraging the positive correlation between MVPA and sleep duration to indirectly support your sleep goal.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you take at least one 5-minute movement break after prolonged seated work today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports taking at least one movement break (walk or stretch) after 60+ minutes of continuous work

assumptions: You can take short breaks during work
You have space to walk or stretch
generated_actions.description: When you complete 60 minutes of continuous seated or focused work, immediately stand up and either walk around for 5 minutes or do a full-body stretch sequence. Look away from your screen.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: When you feel tension rising this afternoon, pause for 2 minutes of box breathing
previous_day_generated_actions.rationale: Your subjective wellbeing shows severe strain: stress score was elevated yesterday (41) and rising (+0.57 slope), while coping capacity is critically low (39) and sharply declining (-9.87 slope). With hard constraints 'tight schedule' and 'high workload', this micro-intervention uses implementation intention ('when you feel tension') to disrupt stress accumulation and support the 'high mood, low function' paradox noted in the holistic report.

previous_day_generated_actions.description: If you notice stress or tension building during your high-workload afternoon, immediately stop and do 4 rounds of box breathing: inhale 4 seconds, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Focus on your breath.

Meal composition included processed foods (croissant, chips, cookie). Habit-stacking lunch planning to your morning coffee routine directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag and the soft constraint 'caution with high energy expenditure given recent calorie/protein deficits', aiming to stabilize energy and support recovery.

previous_day_generated_actions.title: Set a 7:30 PM 'eating cutoff' alarm to finish dinner by 7:00 PM

This aligns with excellent sleep efficiency (93.27%) and low wake ratio (6.73%) per the 'improved_nutrition_timing_with_sleep_quality' cross-domain signal. Reinforcing this pattern with an external cue directly supports your sleep goal domain, the 'sleep_consistency_recovery' priority, and the soft constraint 'maintain earlier eating window (<1200 min) to support sleep'.

evaluation.success_definition: Last meal occurs before 7:00 PM (time_last_meal_minutes < 1020) AND user reports the alarm was helpful

assumptions: You have a phone or alarm device
You can adjust your evening schedule
previous_day_generated_actions.description: Set an alarm on your phone for 7:30 PM tonight. When it rings, that's your signal to stop any eating and begin winding down. Aim to actually finish dinner by 7:00 PM local time.

must_avoid: continuing screen use after the alarm

This tiny break directly addresses the 'activity_decline_amid_recovery_rebound' cross-domain signal and the soft constraint 'consider gentle activity to counter sedentary trend' within your 'tight schedule' and 'high workload' hard constraints, while leveraging your high subjective energy mode.

previous_day_briefing.morning_message:
Morning Ameena.

You're feeling fantastic today — that 100 score and high energy mode is real. Let's start with last night: you slept **6.93 hours** with 93% efficiency and very little waking. That's your longest, most efficient sleep recently, and your body's recovery signals are showing a dramatic rebound — your HRV hit **120**, which is exceptionally strong. That's a great foundation.

But there's a striking tension here. Yesterday, despite your body recovering well, your subjective energy was critically low at **30**, focus at **39**, and stress was rising. This is the 4th day of this mismatch: your physiological readiness is climbing while your internal experience feels depleted. Your activity also dipped yesterday — MVPA was just 16 minutes, and you were more sedentary. The data suggests your body might be using this lower activity to recover, but it's leaving you feeling drained.

Today's theme is about bridging that gap. Your actions are designed to protect that strong recovery while gently addressing the internal strain — using tiny breaks to manage stress and planning your nutrition to support your energy.

You've built a solid recovery base; today's about making sure your inner experience matches it.

evidence_quotes: I couldn't understand my EM physics homework
He misses me off so much

meal_descriptions.description: Egg and cheese muffins with 2 fried eggs, a hashbrown, a chicken sausages and a small iced latte
Acai bowl with blueberries, strawberries and chocolate granola
Greek yogurt protein drink and a banana
A slice if pizza and 1/4 jerk chicken with bone and roasted potatoes and a side salad
